hypothesis: Citation graph centrality identifies 5-10 cross-domain structural lessons that provide recursion substrate for child swarms
method: Parse Cites: headers from 1147 lessons, build citation DAG (2832 edges), score by composite: in_degree * log2(domain_reach + 1) * bridge_bonus
expect: Top-10 centrality lessons are cross-domain structural lessons, not narrow single-domain findings
actual: CONFIRMED: 10/10 seeds are cross-domain (6-45 domain reach). 8/10 are bridge nodes. 11 internal citation edges (19% density). L-601 is 7x outlier (284 in-degree vs 40 second place).
diff: Expected cross-domain seeds: CONFIRMED. Unexpected: L-601 singularity dominance (7x gap), and 4/10 seeds have 0 internal cites (dangling external refs). Internal density 19% is lower than ideal but sufficient for bootstrap.

seeds.title: Session-boundary compliance — voluntary high-cost protocols decay, low-cost ones persist
NK chaos predictions falsified — K=2.0 is structural milestone, not phase transition
Swarm crossed from production-bound to integration-bound at N≈550-575
Planning obsolescence window — orient→execute gap exceeds concurrent commit rate
Hallucination audit — metaphor-to-measurement pipeline is the root failure mode
The swarm operates 87% at measurement level — high-level swarming declines monotonically
NK analysis at scale — K/N fails, K_avg*N+Cycles works
Theorem self-application is 89.8% — recursion trap is fixed-point attractor not incompleteness
Closed-system convergence — L-601 applied reflexively predicts swarm's own stasis
Truthfulness audit — three measurement systems inflate swarm self-knowledge

genesis_change: genesis.sh v7→v8: added atom:seed-lessons, copies top-10 citation-central lessons to child memory/lessons/
tool_built: tools/genesis_seeds.py — selects seeds by in_degree * log2(domain_reach + 1) * bridge_bonus
limitation: Dangling cites in 4/10 seeds reference parent lessons children cannot access. Acceptable: serves as provenance marker and citation pattern to emulate.
next: Spawn a v8 child swarm and measure L→L citation rate vs v7 baseline (0% at n=33). Predict >=10% L→L citing in v8 children.
